data.title: Lilly signs licensing deal with Mediar for IPF drug
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has entered into a licensing agreement with Mediar Therapeutics to advance Mediar's drug MTX-463 into Phase 2 testing for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

Mediar said it expects to initiate a Phase 2 study in the first half of 2025. After the study is completed, Lilly (NYSE:LLY) will have the right to lead further development and commercialization of the product, according to a statement.

Under the deal, Mediar will receive $99M in upfront and near-term payments and could be eligible for up to $687M in additional milestone payments. Mediar would also be eligible to receive high-single to low-double digit royalty payments and net sales milestones based on potential future product sales.

data.title: Handy Technologies settles with FTC for deceptive practices - updated
data.description:
Updated Jan 10 13:09ET to clarify that Handy Technologies, doing business as Angi Services, agreed to a settlement with the FTC, and includes statement from a company spokesperson.

Handy Technologies, doing business as Angi Services (NASDAQ:ANGI), agreed to a settlement with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and ordered to make “substantial” changes to its business practices for misleading contractors on its platform regarding their earning potential, charging excessive fees for timely payments, and imposing fines on contractors for jobs that were cancelled by the requester.

The FTC complaint charges that Handy (NASDAQ:ANGI), advertised inflated hourly fees for gig jobs that “far outstrip any reasonable amount a worker can expect to be paid,” charged an additional fee to be paid as soon as the job is done, and other fees and fines that the company failed to adequately disclose.

“New York workers deserve to be paid what they are promised, when they are promised,” said New York Attorney General Letitia James, adding that “Apps like Handy’s offer New Yorkers flexible job opportunities, but they cannot be allowed to lure workers with lies and false promises.”

Under the terms of the settlement, Handy (NASDAQ:ANGI) would be required to pay $2.95M to refund harmed contractors and make changes to its platform to ensure contractors give clear consent to any fees that are charged by the company, as well as provide clear instructions how to avoid fines. The settlement would also require Handy (NASDAQ:ANGI) to back up any claims about how much contractors can potentially make.

"Though we were prepared to litigate, we chose to enter into an agreement with these parties to put this matter to rest and get back to putting our 100% focus on supporting our customers. None of the agencies' allegations were fair, and this settlement should in no way be construed as a validation of their allegations," a company spokesperson told Seeking Alpha.

This is the second time in less than two years that a company affiliated with Angi (NASDAQ:ANGI) agreed to settle with the FTC. In April 2023, the agency charged HomeAdvisor with making false, misleading, or unsubstantiated claims about the quality and source of the leads it sells to service providers and for misleading service providers as to how much those projects would pay. HomeAdvisor agreed to pay $7.2M for redress and agreed from making any further false or misleading claims regarding its leads.

data.title: S&P 500 turns red for 2025 as hot jobs data sparks recalibration of rate cut bets
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday erased all the gains made in the fledgling year, after a hotter-than-expected jobs report led to analysts and traders significantly dialing back their odds of further Federal Reserve rate cuts.

The three major averages had bounced off their session lows, but were still deep in negative territory. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -1.01% at 5,858.28 points in afternoon trade, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -1.22% at 42,113.03 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.10% at 19,264.99 points.

Nine of the 11 S&P sectors were in the red.

Before the opening bell, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported 256K nonfarm payroll additions to the economy in December 2024, easily outstripping the consensus of +157K. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.1%, coming in lower than the expected figure of 4.2%.

The data suggested that the labor market has once again become highly resilient after showing considerable cooling in the middle of last year. For a Fed that wants to ease monetary policy, the jobs report was another spanner in the works.

"After only reluctantly cutting (rates) last month, we think it would take a very bad set of jobs reports to get the (Fed's monetary policy committee) easing again by March, and, so, we now see the next cut in June followed by a final one in September," JPMorgan's Michael Feroli said.

Goldman Sachs followed JPMorgan in predicting only two more interest rate cuts this year. Bank of America went one step further and said it sees no more rate cuts at all, adding that there appeared to be greater risks toward policymakers now raising rates.

U.S. Treasury yields surged as market participants dumped bonds after the jobs report. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) flirted with 4.80%, a level not seen since the end of October 2023. It was last up 8 basis points to 4.77%. The shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 12 basis points to 4.39%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

U.S. yields have soared amid an extended bond selloff since the Fed began its rate-cutting cycle in September 2024, partly driven by concerns over inflation and uncertainty over incoming President Donald Trump's policies. But BTIG's Jonathan Krinsky made a case that the reaction to today's employment report meant a "tactical top."

"The best tell in markets for when something is priced in is a news failure. That occurs when news comes out, yet prices move opposite to what would be expected given that news. In this case, we're talking about bonds, which have been in a significant decline in recent weeks based in part to better than expected economic data," Krinsky said.

"The knee-jerk reaction to today's employment data was lower, but we are always more interested in the 'reaction to the reaction'. If long-end yields can close lower today, we can say the recent move was in anticipation of this number, and we likely have a tactical top in yields. If that's the case, then we likely have a tactical low in stocks, although we continue to think we have unfinished business lower later this quarter," the technician added.

Though the jobs report dominated Friday's headlines, investors also paid attention to some high-profile quarterly results. Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) soared +28% after delivering a bottom-line beat amid a turnaround plan that is gaining traction.

Conversely, Constellation Brands (STZ) plunged -16.2% after the Corona beer giant missed top- and bottom-line expectations.

Delta Air Lines (DAL) climbed +8.9% after a strong holiday travel season helped the largest U.S. carrier deliver record fourth quarter revenue and operating profit.

data.title: Bank of America set to snap six days of gains as shares trade in red
data.description:
Shares of Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) were on track to snap six straight sessions of gains, as the stock fell more than 2% in afternoon trade.

The American banking giant gained more than 5% in the last six trading sessions. The stock gained more than 35% last year, outperforming the broader S&P 500 Index, which gained more than 24%.

BAC is up 2% since the beginning of the year. The stock closed 0.28% up, at $46.21 on Wednesday.

Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) was upgraded to Buy by both HSBC and UBS on Tuesday, citing the higher-for-longer rate environment as benefiting the money center bank.

For Bank of America, "the recent (stock) pullback provides an opportunity to add exposure to a market leader with an attractive earnings and profitability outlook at an attractive valuation," wrote HSBC's Saul Martinez in a note to clients.

Wolfe Research also shared a positive outlook for the banking giant earlier this month, choosing Bank of America as one of its top financial picks for 2025. According to the analysts at Wolfe, there are a number of tailwinds that support outperformance for the bank.

Despite positive views from some analysts, Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating has given BAC a cautious HOLD rating, with a score of 3.37 out of 5. SA authors are however optimistic and have rated the stock a BUY.

The sell-side analysts also see the stock as a BUY with 18 out of 23 analysts recommending BUY and above.

Seeking Alpha analyst On the Pulse believes that Bank of America is set for a strong 2025 due to the Fed's higher-for-longer rate environment, boosting net interest income.

The analyst also adds that the bank’s Consumer Banking segment thrives with robust growth, which positions it for cyclical profit growth.

“Despite a 25% premium to book value, Bank of America's strong results and favorable economic conditions make it a top bank investment for 2025,” the analyst added.

data.title: Eli Lilly in talks to acquire Scorpion Therapeutics, FT says; Relay Therapeutics surges
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is in advanced discussions to purchase cancer-focused biotech Scorpion Therapeutics for as much as $2.5 billion.

Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is expected to pay around $1 billion upfront for the privately owned Scorpion, according to a Financial Times report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Lilly will pay as much as an additional $1.5 billion at a later day if the biotech hits certain milestones.

Scorpion has a drug candidate that inhibits a mutation generally viewed as partly responsible for breast, gynecological, and head and neck cancers.

Eli Lilly (LLY) and Scorpion didn't immediately respond to the FT's requests for comment.

Shares of Relay Therapeutics (RLAY), which makes similar technology to Scorpion for fighting cancer, jumped 22% on Lilly news.

data.title: Stellantis lowers U.S. inventory by 100K by end of 2024
data.description:
• As part of its efforts to “enlarge its remediation efforts” to lower vehicle inventory though reduced shipments and increased incentives on older models, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) has achieved its goal to trim U.S. vehicle inventory by more than 100,000 by the end of 2024.
  • The reduction “represents a very big improvement that cost us a lot, but was needed,” Stellantis’ head of North American operations was quoted by Reuters saying at the Detroit Auto Show.
  • In September, while still under the direction of former CEO Carlos Tavares, and as part of its guidance revision for 2024, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) said it planned to accelerate inventory normalization through increased incentives on 2024 and older model years and “productivity improvement initiatives that encompass both cost and capacity adjustments.”
  • The strategy was aimed to address the deterioration in the global auto industry and increased competition from China.

data.title: J&J gets EU okay for Dual Energy Thermocool Smarttouch Catheter
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has received EU approval for its Dual Energy Thermocool Smarttouch SF Catheter for the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias.

The product is an irrigated, contact-force sensing catheter powered by J&J's (NYSE:JNJ) Trupulse Generator and is fully integrated with the Carto 3 System for electro-anatomical mapping and for tag indexing.

J&J (JNJ) expects Trupulse Generator hardware compatibility in the first half of 2025. When fully approved, the platform will provide electrophysiologists with the ability to switch between radiofrequency and pulsed field energy in the same catheter, according to a statement.

data.title: Nokia shares snap six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Nokia (NYSE:NOK) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock was down 2.6% at $4.54 on Friday.

The global technology company added 5.2% in the preceding six sessions. The stock advanced nearly 30% in 2024, dwarfing the 24% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

NOK is up 3% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NOK has a Hold rating with a score of 3.1 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, B in momentum, while it got a D- growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 6 analysts gave NOK a Buy and above, while five gave it a Hold rating. No analyst has recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Most recently, Nokia (NYSE:NOK) and Openreach introduced intent-based networking to build an open-access network, accelerating UK fiber broadband rollout.

Earlier in December 2024, Nokia was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral by J.P. Morgan due in part to a recovery in telecommunications spending in 2025 and the Finnish company's newly expanded partnership with Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT). "Nokia should also grow in the enterprise market, E.G. the recent expansion of its relationship with Microsoft Cloud."

“Nokia has a strong asset and patent portfolio, but its competitive edge is limited, especially against rivals like Ericsson,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: Psychedelic drug developer Lykos could get lifeline from former Tesla board member
data.description:
Lykos Therapeutics, the developer of psychedelic drugs for mental health conditions that has been in financial difficulty, could get rescued by a former Tesla (TSLA) board member seen as close to Elon Musk.

Antonio Gracias, who also supported Musk's acquisition of Twitter, has made an offer to take a controlling stake in Lykos, which is privately held, in exchange for $30M in equity and $70M in secured debt, according to the Financial Times. He is also seeking three board seats, with the other two going to the Multidisciplinary Association for Psychedelic Studies, which founded the company.

Lykos laid off 75% of its workforce in August 2024 after the U.S. FDA declined to approve midomafetamine, an MDMA-based drug to treat PTSD. The FDA asked the company for an independent review of its prior trials and to conduct a new study.

Gracias' strategy is to have Paragon Biosciences help Lykos get midomafetamine approved and then launch it, the FT reported, citing sources.

MAPS President Rick Doblin has backed the plan as well.

However, Lykos' principal shareholder, Helena Special Investments, has also offered a plan that would provide Lykos a bridge loan of $20M-$30M, according to the newspaper.

Lykos' board is slated to meet Friday to discuss both proposals.

Publicly traded psychedelic-focused drug developers include Mind Medicine (NASDAQ:MNMD), Atai Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATAI), COMPASS Pathways (NASDAQ:CMPS), Cybin (NYSE:CYBN), and GH Research (GHRS).

Nano-caps include 180 Life Sciences (ATNF), Enveric Biosciences (ENVB), Seelos Therapeutics (SEEL), SciSparc (SPRC), Bright Minds Biosciences (DRUG), and Clearmind Medicine (CMND).

data.title: Gene editing stocks drop as Intellia announces layoffs
data.description:
Shares of gene editing companies dropped on Friday as Wall Street reacted to Intellia Therapeutics' (NASDAQ:NTLA) plans to lay off 27% of its staff alongside a decision to halt a mid-stage clinical program targeted at a rare genetic disorder.

While Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) reached a new 52-week low, its rivals, LENZ Therapeutics (LENZ), Verve Therapeutics (VERV), Prime Medicine (PRME), Editas Medicine (EDIT), Sangamo Therapeutics (SGMO), and CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) were also among notable decliners.

The Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech's latest round of layoffs coincided with its decision to halt the development of its CRISPR candidate NTLA-3001, which is targeted at alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency-associated lung disease.

It comes roughly a year after the company's previous strategic review, which led to the departure of approximately 15% of its workforce and a pause in certain research-stage programs.

Despite an $8M charge attributed to the reorganization in Q1, Intellia (NTLA) said its cash balance of ~$862M at the end of Q4 2024, as well as cost savings generated from layoffs, would extend its cash runway through H1 2027 when its first U.S. commercial launch is anticipated.

Intellia (NTLA) is not the only gene editing firm to lay off staff in recent weeks. Its CRISPR rival Editas Medicine (EDIT) waved goodbye to approximately 65% of its staff in December as it shelved reni-cel, its lead gene-editing candidate for sickle cell disease, after a commercial partner didn't come forward to develop it.
